title: Indexes, Query Plans, Operator Costs, and Responsible Tuning · Independent Query Trial
summary: A growing ticket dataset makes queue and analytics queries slow, but each new index adds write and storage cost. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an evidence-backed query performance dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain B-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs.
Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Index Structures
steps.instruction:
lab case — Queue queries filter status and severity, order by opened_at, and join owner_id across 6000 modeled rows. Compare baseline and indexed plans, preserve results, and account for insert and storage cost.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Performance Experiment
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-performance-experiment-1vwm90" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; index, access-path, plan, or controlled-performance evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Performance Experiment.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Performance Experiment into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Performance Experiment, keep Performance Experiment connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-performance-experiment-1vwm90
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id FROM tickets WHERE owner_id = 1 ORDER BY opened_at;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction

Compare baseline and indexed plans, preserve results, and account for insert and storage cost. Competency probe: Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.
